chunk_summary: The 24-hour bowl recall intake mini-form is a structured tool for assessing complementary feeding in children aged 6–24 months, focusing on texture, amount, schedule, and caregiver interaction to guide clinical decisions and follow-up.
learning_objectives: Understand the steps of conducting a 24-hour bowl recall for complementary feeding assessment.
Identify key feeding issues such as texture delays, excessive milk intake, or unsafe practices.
Learn how to document Minimum Dietary Diversity (MDD), Minimum Meal Frequency (MMF), and Minimum Acceptable Diet (MAD) indicators.
Apply measurable changes to improve feeding practices and monitor progress during follow-up visits.
key_facts: The 24-hour bowl recall quantifies feeding texture, amount, schedule, and caregiver interaction.
Use a 250 mL cup estimate if the household bowl volume is unknown.
Rapid flags include meals >30 minutes, milk intake >500 mL/day, and unsafe storage/unboiled water.
MDD target is ≥5 out of 8 food groups; MMF requires age-appropriate solid/semi-solid/soft feeds.
MAD combines MDD and MMF; non-breastfed children need ≥2 milk feeds daily.
reference_quote: Faltering growth: recognition and management (NG75) — NICE: 'Use structured tools to assess feeding practices in children with weight faltering or other nutritional concerns.'
document_key_topics: Indicators for dietary diversity and meal frequency
